indicator_text: CPI total score (0–100, sum of 48 HG/CPI item scores)
scoring_rule: Sum of all 48 CPI questions; max attainable = 100. Threefold classification: 0–29 lowly regulated; 30–59 medium regulated; 60–100 highly regulated.
source_quote: the maximum attainable number of points is 100 [papers/text/Bednarova_2020__lobbying_regulation_quality.txt:222]; "systems with low lobbying regulation achieve 1–29 points, systems with medium regulation 30–59 points and a highly regulated systems 60 points and more" [papers/text/Bednarova_2020__lobbying_regulation_quality.txt:240-243]
notes: Composite score for benefits side of the Ninefold theory. CZ current = 0, CZ draft = 34, Austria = 38, Slovenia = 41, Poland = 31, Slovakia = 6, Hungary = 0.

indicator_text: CII total score, rescaled to 0–100 (raw 0–288 divided by 2.88)
scoring_rule: Sum of all 47 CII questions; raw range 0–288. Rescaled by dividing by 2.88 to fit 0–100. CII Threefold theory: 1–29 low burden; 30–69 medium burden; 70–100 high burden.
source_quote: The reached values of the index are in the range 0–288 points... it is necessary to unite the scale at the interval 0–100 points, which can be achieved by a simple division of the CII value by 2.88 [papers/text/Bednarova_2020__lobbying_regulation_quality.txt:300-309]; "low regulatory burden (CII in the range of 1–29 points), systems with a medium burden of lobbying regulation (CII value amounting to 30–69 points) and highly burdened systems in terms of costs (CII value in the range 70–100 points)" [papers/text/Bednarova_2020__lobbying_regulation_quality.txt:312-318]
notes: Composite score for costs side of the Ninefold theory. CZ current = 0, CZ draft = 35 (rescaled, raw ~100.8), Austria = 49, Slovenia = 44, Poland = 34, Slovakia = 10, Hungary = 0. Tab. 2 total cell shows "100/2,88 / 35" — i.e. raw 100, rescaled 35 (rounded; 100/2.88 = 34.72).
